MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Apr-13-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Allison B. Surowitz, Esq., of SELMAN BREITMAN (415-979-0400), appearing for defendant / moving party Sepco Corporation
Oren P. Noah, Esq., of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party / opposing party Jeff Bradshaw

RULING:
DEFENDANT SEPCO CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Argued and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Sepco Corporation's motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff did not present any admissible evidence that he was exposed to an asbestos-containing product attributable to defendant. Defendant's D'Amico objections to the declaration of Mr. Jimenez regarding the asbestos content of the materials he worked with at the Shell facility and the time frame he worked with Mr. Bradshaw at the Tosco facility directly contradict Mr. Jimenez's deposition testimony in his own case and the contradictions are not explained in the declaration. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn, Reporter: LaVena Ward, CSR # 7077 = (220/HEK)

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Mar-30-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Jasun C. Molinelli, Esq. of ARCHER NORRIS (925-930-6600), appearing for defendant / moving party Albay Construction Company
Oren Noah, Esq. of BRAYTON & PURCELL (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiffs / opposing parties Jeff Bradshaw, et al.

RULING:
DEFENDANT ALBAY CONSTRUCTION CO.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Tentative ruling adopted: The moving party submits to the tentative ruling. Defendant Albay Construction Co.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. By failing to include a response to a special interrogatory asking plaintiff for all facts supporting his contentions that defendant caused him to be exposed to asbestos, defendant cannot successfully invoke the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. JUDGE: HAROLD KAHN, REPORTER: LAVENA WARD, CSR #7707. =(220/HEK)

Off Calendar Jun-29-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearance and tentative ruling adopted: DEFENDANT TOSCO REFINING COMPANY, INC.'s Joinder In Warren Pump'S Motion For Protective Order And To Extend The Time For Plaintiff's Deposition. Defendant Warren Pumps' motion for a protective order for additional time to depose plaintiff and all joinders thereto are granted. All named defendants may have additional time for Mr. Bradshaw's deposition up to a total (including time already elapsed) of 30 hours. Plaintiff's opposition acknowledges the need for additional deposition time by stating that 'The remaining information left to cover in deposition is the rest of the automotive friction work for Mr. Bradshaw, client specific questions for defendants, and Mr. Bradshaw's medical history and damages.' Given the number of defendants in the case, the request for ten additional hours beyond that specified in GO 129 is reasonable. The parties are to meet and confer regarding scheduling in an effort to reduce the discomfort to Mr. Bradshaw of the additional deposition hours. Because this case was filed over two and a half years ago, the request that plaintiff disclose any intent to sue additional defendants is also granted.
